commit.message:
Pre-live hardening: safety + reliability improvements (#2)

* Pre-live hardening: 7 safety/reliability improvements

- Absolute file paths via _BASE_DIR so bot works from any cwd
- Atomic save_state (write-temp → rename) prevents corrupt state on crash
- OHLCV NaN/None/Inf/negative validation before numpy operations
- State deserialization type+range validation (_load_position helper)
- Dead man's switch watchdog thread (os._exit if no heartbeat in 120s)
- Telegram alerting wired up for entry/exit/errors (gated by TELEGRAM_ENABLED)
- VWAP: fix docstring (rolling N-bar, not session-reset), configurable via vwap_lookback_bars

All 46 tests pass, 0 flake8 violations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address review feedback + fix coverage gate

- Move _kick_watchdog() outside if success: block — guards against hangs,
  not just successful cycles (P1 from Gemini + Codex)
- Narrow send_telegram exception to (URLError, TimeoutError) per Gemini review
- Add test_striker_utils.py covering validate_ohlcv_arrays, _load_position,
  send_telegram no-op, and atomic save_state/load_state round-trips
- Coverage: 58.6% → 76.3% (21 new tests; 67 total pass)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix unused imports in test_striker_utils (flake8 F401)

commit.message:
fix: add pipeline-level dedup to prevent duplicate executions

Same whale signal from overlapping poll responses was triggering 3+
copy trades, inflating consecutive losses and poisoning the circuit
breaker. Dedup key is conditionId:side with 5min TTL — one execution
per contract side per window.

feat: add outcome learning, event calendar, TF-IDF linking, and order book depth

commit.message:
Fix Sebring — restore Rules Search card to dashboard grid

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

WAL checkpoint markers, crash recovery fixes, coverage & ADR updates (#52)

* Add segmented WAL rotation design doc

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add segmented WAL rotation implementation plan (9 tasks)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(persist): segmented WAL with rotation + truncation

Replace the single-file WAL with a directory of byte-size-capped segment
files. Each segment is self-describing (schema preamble), enabling
independent deletion without rewriting the active file.

New API:
- WalConfig { max_segment_bytes } — configurable segment size (default 64MB)
- Wal::create(dir, codecs, config) / Wal::open(dir, codecs, config)
- Wal::delete_segments_before(seq) — caller-driven truncation
- Wal::segment_count() / Wal::oldest_seq() — introspection
- WalCursor lazily advances across segment boundaries
- WalError::CursorBehind returned when requested seq was truncated

Breaking change: WAL is now a directory, not a file. Pre-1.0, zero users.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add WAL checkpoint markers design doc

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add WAL checkpoint markers implementation plan (7 tasks)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(persist): WAL checkpoint markers with CheckpointHandler

- WalEntry::Checkpoint { snapshot_seq } variant in WAL stream
- WalConfig::max_bytes_between_checkpoints (None = disabled)
- Wal::checkpoint_needed() / last_checkpoint_seq() / acknowledge_snapshot()
- scan_active_segment recovers checkpoint state on open
- WalCursor + replay_from skip checkpoint entries transparently
- CheckpointHandler trait + AutoCheckpoint default (saves snapshot + ack)
- Durable::with_checkpoint() fires handler after successful transact
- Backward compatible: Durable::new() works without handler

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(persist): Wal::open falls back to active_start_seq when no mutations found

After rollover + delete_segments_before(u64::MAX), the active segment
may contain only a schema preamble with no mutations, and all earlier
segments are gone. Without this fix, next_seq stays at 0 on reopen,
reusing already-issued sequence numbers and breaking replay/cursor
ordering guarantees.

The active segment's start_seq was assigned from next_seq at rollover
time, so it's the minimum safe value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(persist): rewrite schema preamble after crash truncates active segment to empty

If a crash tears the schema frame during roll_segment, scan_active_segment
truncates the active file to byte 0. Without this fix, subsequent appends
write mutation frames into a segment without a schema preamble, making it
non-self-describing — cross-process replay with different component ID
registration order fails because remap has no schema to build from.

Now Wal::open detects active_bytes == 0 after recovery and rewrites the
schema preamble before accepting appends.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(persist): don't fail append when post-write segment rollover fails

The mutation frame is already flushed and next_seq incremented before
roll_segment runs. If rollover fails (ENOSPC, name collision), returning
Err is misleading — the write is durable. Callers like Durable treat WAL
errors as fatal panics, and retrying would duplicate the logical operation.

Rollover failure is now silently ignored. The oversized segment remains
valid and the next append retries the roll.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(persist): address PR review — atomicity, cross-segment recovery, error handling

Critical fixes:
- roll_segment is now atomic: schema preamble is written to a local
  file handle before updating self fields. A failed preamble write
  no longer leaves Wal pointing at an empty segment.
- Checkpoint state (last_checkpoint_seq, bytes_since_checkpoint) is
  now recovered from sealed segments during open(), not just the
  active segment. Fixes bytes_since_checkpoint under-counting after
  restart with multi-segment WALs.

Important fixes:
- CheckpointHandler::on_checkpoint_needed now returns Result instead
  of requiring expect(). AutoCheckpoint propagates I/O errors instead
  of panicking. Durable::transact silently ignores checkpoint handler
  errors (transaction is already committed and applied).
- Fix stale file-level comment ("WalRecord" → "WalEntry").
- Fix stale "legacy WAL" comment in apply_record.
- Rename _last_start_seq → last_start_seq, _next_pos → next_pos
  (underscore-prefixed but used).

Suggestions:
- acknowledge_snapshot asserts seq <= next_seq to catch bogus future
  sequence numbers.
- Add cursor_reads_across_segment_boundaries test (exercises
  try_advance_segment with small segments).
- Add cursor_behind_after_segment_deletion test (exercises
  CursorBehind error after delete_segments_before).
- Add checkpoint_recovered_from_sealed_segment test (verifies
  cross-segment checkpoint recovery on open).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: improve branch coverage for transaction.rs (77→89%) and reducer.rs (80→82%)

transaction.rs:
- Conflict::display_with empty names fallback
- Tx::read, Tx::remove, Tx::has_locks (optimistic + pessimistic)
- SequentialTx::insert/remove/get_mut/despawn
- Pessimistic exhausted retries returns Conflict

reducer.rs:
- EntityRef::entity() accessor
- QueryMut::for_each_chunk
- ReducerId::index() / QueryReducerId::index()
- ReducerRegistry::access() / query_reducer_access()
- ReducerRegistry Default impl

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update ADR-008 with segmented WAL + checkpoints, add ADR-015 for replication

ADR-008: fold in segmented WAL (directory of segment files, rollover,
caller-driven truncation), checkpoint markers (WalEntry::Checkpoint,
CheckpointHandler trait, AutoCheckpoint), and the durable-vs-operational
error classification principle.

ADR-015: new ADR for pull-based WAL replication — WalCursor, lazy
segment advancement, ReplicationBatch with self-describing schema,
CursorBehind for re-bootstrap after truncation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
security: 全面補強 ERP DB 唯讀保護

1. 修正 pool alias 衝突
   - database-oracle.js: poolAlias='system_db'
   - dashboardService.js: poolAlias='erp_db'
   - 兩個 pool 之前都沒有 alias，都用預設'default'，
     第二個建立時會觸發 NJS-037 衝突

2. ReadOnlyConnectionProxy 補齊所有危險方法封鎖
   - 之前只代理 execute/close，其餘方法可透過 this._conn 洩漏
   - 新增封鎖: executeMany, commit, rollback, changePassword,
     shutdown, startup, getSodaDatabase, getQueue, subscribe,
     createLob, beginSessionlessTransaction,
     resumeSessionlessTransaction, suspendSessionlessTransaction,
     tpcBegin, tpcCommit, tpcRollback
   - queryStream 允許但同樣過 assertErpReadOnly 驗證
   - 採白名單策略: 只暴露 execute/queryStream/close

refactor: auditoría general — bugs, DRY, UX

- FormHeader.tsx: header back+título reutilizable (antes duplicado en 8 vistas)

Fase 2 – Bugs corregidos

  estado local (compras, ventas, gastos, bonos, inventario)

- Bug C: elimina setLoading(true) antes de loadData() post-guardado para
  evitar spinner de pantalla completa innecesario (7 páginas)

Fase 3 – Refactor DRY
- 3A: FORMAS_PAGO desde constants.ts en compras, ventas, dashboard
- 3B: <Spinner fullPage /> reemplaza HTML inline en 8 páginas
- 3C: <FormHeader> reemplaza header inline en 8 vistas de formulario
- 3D: formatFormaPago() reemplaza .replace('_',' ') en 5 call sites
- Bonus: detail view spinners en clientes/proveedores → <Spinner size="sm" />

Fase 4 – UX: búsqueda en Gastos
- Barra de búsqueda por concepto, categoría, notas, persona
- Subtitle dinámico "N de M registros" cuando hay filtro activo
- totalMes sigue calculándose sobre todos los gastos (no filtrado)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chat: paint loop performance optimizations

- P0: Cache parseMessageElements() on message object during layout,
  reuse in paint/act instead of re-parsing 10+ regexes per frame
- P1: Cache color-coded + shadow lines per message, keyed by hover
  state — eliminates ~400 substring calls and ~40 regex replacements
  per frame when nothing changes
- P2: Cache text.width() for timestamps and hearts — ~37 fewer O(n)
  measurements per frame
- P3: Fix stripInlineColorCodes() from O(n^2) to O(n) single-pass
- P4: Throttle timeAgo() to 1-second intervals per message

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Separate account and settings into clean stacked popovers

popover with back-navigation. Added backdrop overlay, entrance
animations, proper z-index layering, and max-height containment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): remove gui from default features to prevent Skia on all targets

nih_plug_xtask passes --features X to cargo without --no-default-features,
so default features always compile alongside whatever CI specifies. Since
gui is in defaults, Skia (skia-bindings) compiled for every target even
when explicitly omitted — causing x86 SIMD cross-compile failures on ARM64.

Removing gui from defaults lets CI control Skia inclusion per matrix entry.
Local bundle commands already specify all features explicitly via justfile
and the install docs, so this has no effect on local GUI development.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent poison messages from blocking WAL flush permanently

One bad message (FK constraint violation, invalid data) in the WAL would
fail the entire transaction, causing the room to re-enter degraded mode
on every /repair retry. Now inserts are individual — constraint errors
(23xxx) are logged and skipped, while transient errors (connection,
timeout) still trigger retry/degraded logic. Also fixed console.log
printf-style format strings (unsupported in CF Workers) and improved
PG error code extraction from Drizzle's wrapped errors.

Filosofia3, SelfModel, Computational Thermodynamics, Epistemic Tomography,
Tri-Brain, Causal Complexity, GNN Routing, DAM) are now clearly attributed
to Felipe Maya Muniz as original inventions from the ATIC project.

notes on what is original vs what is adapted from prior work.
